visits.note: Patient presents with complaints of fatigue and polyuria. Physical examination reveals no signs of distress. HbA1c is elevated at 7.2%, BP is slightly elevated at 140/90 mmHg. Patient is diagnosed with type 2 diabetes and hypertension. Medications initiated: Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg OD.

Follow-up visit 6 months post-initial diagnosis. Patient reports improvement in fatigue but persistent polyuria. Physical examination reveals no significant changes. HbA1c has improved to 6.8% and BP is slightly improved to 135/85 mmHg. Patient's LDL is elevated at 140. Medications adjusted: Metformin 1000mg BID, Lisinopril 20mg OD. Simvastatin 20mg OD initiated for hyperlipidemia.

Follow-up visit 12 months post-initial diagnosis. Patient reports worsening fatigue and polyuria. Physical examination reveals pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities. HbA1c has worsened to 7.5% and BP is elevated at 145/95 mmHg. Patient's eGFR has decreased to 65. Medications adjusted: Metformin 1500mg BID, Lisinopril 40mg OD. Furosemide 20mg OD initiated for fluid overload.

Follow-up visit 20 months post-initial diagnosis. Patient reports worsening fatigue and polyuria. Physical examination reveals worsening edema in bilateral lower extremities. HbA1c has worsened to 8.2% and BP is elevated at 155/105 mmHg. Patient's eGFR has decreased to 55. Medications adjusted: Metformin 2000mg BID, Lisinopril 80mg OD. Eplerenone 25mg OD initiated for resistant hypertension.

Follow-up visit 23 months post-initial diagnosis. Patient reports worsening fatigue and polyuria. Physical examination reveals end-stage renal disease. HbA1c is stable at 8.2% but BP is elevated at 160/110 mmHg. Patient's eGFR has decreased to 20. Medications adjusted: Hemodialysis initiated for end-stage renal disease. Losartan 100mg OD initiated for resistant hypertension.
